section: Abstract
text: The (n,p) reaction on $^{35}\mathrm{Cl}$ will produce $^{35}\mathrm{S}$ at a mean concentration up to a few thousand ppM in the salt (4) which depends upon the isotopic concentration of the chlorine e.g. separated Cl-37.

text: Recently interesting results concerning sulphur chemical states have been published $^{(18,19)}$ .

section: Abstract
text: Using different methods of $^{35}\mathrm{S}-$ species separation and especially non-aqueous medium it was possible to identify the $\mathbf{S}^{2-}$ and $\mathbf{S}^0$ precursors but not those of the sulphite and sulphate $^{(18)}$ .

section: Abstract
text: It was shown that the oxidising point defects produced in the crystal during the irradiation as V centres or derivatives can oxidise the $^{35}\mathrm{S}$ at the moment of solution.

section: Abstract
text: The aerial oxidation can also be very important in aqueous or ammoniacal carrier-free systems but no oxidation was observed in liquid ammonia-cyanide or aqueous cyanide systems in the presence at least $\mathbf{S}^{2-}$ carrier.

section: Abstract
text: The solution in an acid medium even in the absence of both air and carriers invariably lead to complete conversion of all active sulphur into sulphate $^{(18)}$ .

section: EXPERIMENTAL
text: Sodium chloride "Merck" reagent was heated for 60 hrs. at $200^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$ in an oven under the vacuum.

section: EXPERIMENTAL
text: The dried samples of $100\mathrm{mg}$ sealed in evacuated $(10^{-4}$ torr) quartz tubes were irradiated near the core of the "Saphir" reactor (swimming pool) for different periods at a neutron flux of $5\cdot 10^{12}\mathrm{ncm}^{-2}\mathrm{s}^{-1}$ and $4,3\cdot 10^{12}\mathrm{ncm}^{-2}\mathrm{s}^{-1}$ .

section: EXPERIMENTAL
text: Reactor irradiations were carried out at about (estimated only) $150^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$ and $-186^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$ .

section: EXPERIMENTAL
text: After irradiation the samples were 'cooled' for 8 days to allow the decay of $^{24}\mathrm{Na}$ .

section: EXPERIMENTAL
text: The crushing of the irradiated ampoule was made in a special device from which the air was removed by passing a nitrogen stream containing oxygen of 10 ppM.

section: EXPERIMENTAL
text: After crushing, a gentle stream of nitrogen was allowed to flow for about 10 minutes.

section: EXPERIMENTAL
text: The gases evolved were collected in cooled traps of containing 0,1 NaOH solution.

section: EXPERIMENTAL
text: For the $^{35}\mathrm{S}$ -species separation the chemical method described recently by M.

section: EXPERIMENTAL
text: All measurements were made in duplicate with and without aluminium absorber for discriminating $^{35}\mathrm{S}$ from $^{32}\mathrm{P}$ which was produced by the $^{35}\mathrm{Cl}$ ( $n, \alpha$ ) reaction.

section: Post irradiation high temperature heating
text: The sealed irradiated ampoules were heated in an electric oven at $770^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$ for 2 hrs. and $830^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$ for about 5 minutes and then crushed in a closed system under nitrogen stream.
